commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Matrix helper URL construction so direct Matrix helper requests resolve relative to path-prefixed homeserver URLs and bare helper paths get a private client API prefix.

PR surface: Source +28. Total +28 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source plus a read-only Node URL-constructor check show leading-slash Matrix endpoints drop the homeserver path prefix, while the PR-style relative endpoint preserves it.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A repair worker can add the missing focused regression test on the PR branch without changing product direction or the runtime fix.

target.title: fix(matrix): reverse-proxy path support for Matrix homeserver URLs
target.body:
现有的PR Body如下，请帮我优化：
Summary
Fixes Matrix transport URL construction for homeservers behind reverse proxies
with path prefixes. The apiPrefix public config surface was removed following
ClawSweeper review — the fix now lives entirely at the transport layer.

Problem: new URL("/_matrix/...", "https://host/proxy/base/") in
transport.ts strips the reverse-proxy path because the leading / makes
new URL() resolve against the origin only.
Fix: Strip the leading slash from endpoints so new URL() resolves
relative to the full homeserver base path. Also ensure the homeserver URL
always has a trailing slash so new URL() treats it as a directory, not a
filename.
Scope narrowed: The apiPrefix config surface was removed per
ClawSweeper review (the config was not carried through
resolveMatrixConfigForAccount, MatrixAuth, shared client keying, or
createMatrixClient, making it a no-op for normal runtime paths).
MatrixAuthedHttpClient keeps a private internal apiPrefix constant
(/_matrix/client/v3) for bare-path resolution only.
What changed
File	Change
transport.ts	normalizeEndpoint strips leading / for correct new URL() resolution; performMatrixRequest ensures homeserver URL ends with /
http-client.ts	apiPrefix removed from public params, kept as private DEFAULT_API_PREFIX constant
sdk.ts	apiPrefix parameter removed from MatrixClient constructor
config-schema.ts	apiPrefix field removed
types.ts	apiPrefix removed from MatrixConfig type
bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts	Auto-regenerated
Real behavior proof
Environment: Linux x64, Node 24.13.1. Target homeserver: tuwunel
(Conduit fork) behind openresty reverse proxy at
faas.uat.xydigit.com/faas-function/paas-uat/tuwunel-openclaw-test/.

Steps:

pnpm build
openclaw config set channels.matrix.homeserver "https://faas.uat.xydigit.com/faas-function/paas-uat/tuwunel-openclaw-test/"
openclaw config set channels.matrix.accessToken "<redacted>"
openclaw config set channels.matrix.network.dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork true
openclaw gateway run --bind loopback --port 18789 --force
openclaw channels status --probe
Live output:

Matrix default: enabled, configured, running, connected,
  transport:just now, health:healthy,
  url:https://faas.uat.xydigit.com/faas-function/paas-uat/tuwunel-openclaw-test/,
  works
Unit tests: 1393 passed (121 test files) in extensions/matrix/

Risk checklist
No public config surface change — apiPrefix removed, no user-facing impact
Transport URL construction only — SSRF protections fully in place
Standard homeservers (https://matrix.example.org) unaffected — verified
with and without trailing slash
Path-prefixed homeservers now connect correctly — live-proven against
reverse-proxy Conduit deployment
target.author: papilionidae

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: Papilionidae
